behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff (three collectors -> aggregation/composition -> summary input), not a single-function algorithm. A unit test of one formatter could miss dropped/overwritten source fields during module wiring (module-coordination-probe applies). Rejected System because behavior text is about "content passed" and does not require real network/subprocess/OS behavior; in-process source fakes can reproduce omission bugs. Rejected Agentic because this checks pre-model payload completeness, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/orchestration modules with deterministic fakes for metrics API, issue tracker API, and deployment log reader, plus a spy/fake summary generator. Execute one report run and assert the exact summary-input payload contains all three source sections with expected keys/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/error-propagation across modules (source adapter failure -> section fallback text -> report continues), which is beyond a single local function. Rejected System because behavior text only requires handling "one data source is unavailable" and can be reproduced with an in-process adapter fake raising timeout/connection exceptions; no real OS/network primitives are required to catch the fallback bug. Rejected Agentic because correctness is fallback control flow and section annotation, not AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run report assembly with two successful source fakes and one failing fake (e.g., timeout exception). Assert run does not abort, output includes a `data unavailable` marker specifically for the failed section, and other sections still contain normal data.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary output quality (faithfulness to key values), which is model-judgment dependent and can vary across real model calls; deterministic stubs would hide misrepresentation/hallucination failures. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly tests semantic accuracy of the narrative, not just routing/retry/payload shape around the model call. Rejected Workflow because this behavior references a single summary-generation step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real summary-generation dependency on representative report inputs (including edge-value cases) and evaluate whether generated text preserves required key metrics/issues/deploy facts without contradiction or omission, using a rubric or judge that checks factual alignment to the input.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real external infrastructure: end-to-end delivery to an actual inbox requires real HTTP/API/SMTP behavior and network/service integration. Mocks can pass while real auth, transport, formatting, or provider acceptance fails (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires reaching a real "recipient's inbox." Rejected Agentic as minimum because the asserted outcome here is delivery correctness of the pipeline, not model-quality judgment; any summary step can be deterministic for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled job end-to-end in a staging environment with real source endpoints (or staging equivalents) and real email delivery path to a test inbox. Assert message is received by configured recipients with expected subject/body structure and attached/generated report content.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff/persistence across modules and runs: delivery failure -> local file fallback -> later retry picks up persisted artifact. This is best caught by wiring modules with real local filesystem persistence and controlled mailer failure in-process. Rejected System because behavior does not require proving real provider outage semantics; an unreachable-mailer fake can trigger the same branch and catch missing save/retry logic. Rejected Agentic because correctness is retry/persistence workflow, not AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use real report generation/orchestration with a mailer stub that fails first run and succeeds second run. Persist fallback to a temp filesystem path. Assert first run writes report file and records pending delivery state; simulate next scheduled run and assert retry sends email and clears/archives pending fallback state.
